branding-server-module.signature: { start(port?: number): Promise<{pid: number, port: number}>, stop(): Promise<void>, status(): {running: boolean, pid?: number, port?: number} }
branding-server-module.description: Node.js HTTP server module for serving branding artifacts. Manages lifecycle via PID file at .planning/branding/.server.pid.

node-only.description: Server must use node:http module only. No Python, no new npm dependencies.

pid-lifecycle.description: PID file at .planning/branding/.server.pid must be created on start, removed on stop, and checked on status. Stale PIDs (process not running) must be detected and cleaned up.

graceful-port-conflict.description: If the configured port is in use, the server must report the conflict clearly rather than crashing.

definition.scope: Replace standalone HTML file generation with a lightweight Node.js HTTP server that serves branding artifacts as a directory index, with PID-based lifecycle management.

tasks.acceptance: Server starts on configured port, serves .planning/branding/ as directory

tasks.description: Implement PID file management for server lifecycle
tasks.acceptance: PID file created on start, removed on stop, stale PIDs detected

tasks.description: Update skills/branding/SKILL.md to spawn server instead of generating standalone HTML
tasks.acceptance: Branding skill starts server and opens browser to localhost URL

tasks.description: Write unit tests for server lifecycle
tasks.acceptance: Tests cover start, stop, status, stale PID cleanup, and port conflict

acceptance: Branding server starts and serves artifacts on localhost
PID lifecycle management works correctly (start/stop/status/stale detection)
